item.title: Suspect in killings of 3 elderly men in Hawaii arrested after massive manhunt
item.description: A man wanted in connection with the killings of three elderly men was caught after a massive search of Hawaii's Big Island that had left residents on edge.

item.title: Bondi to testify behind closed doors in House Epstein probe today
item.description: Former Attorney General Pam Bondi will testify before the House Oversight Committee on Friday about her handling of the Epstein files.

item.title: Ebola, hantavirus outbreaks raise questions about Trump's health agency cuts
item.description: Infectious disease specialists say the viruses are unlikely to become pandemics, but some are still raising concerns about the federal health response.

item.title: Data center frenzy is spurring a jobs boomlet for blue-collar workers
item.description: The rush to build thousands of U.S. data centers is driving demand for some workers, though economists project fewer permanent jobs.

item.title: Many artists drop out of Freedom 250 concerts shortly after lineup announced
item.description: Many artists announced for the Freedom 250 concert series in Washington, D.C., this summer, say they won't be performing.

item.title: Claude Lemieux, 4-time Stanley Cup champion, dies at age 60
item.description: The NHL Alumni Association announced Claude Lemieux's death. A cause of death was not immediately available, nor was it clear where Lemieux was when he died.

item.title: Vance says "still TBD" if Trump will sign a U.S.-Iran deal
item.description: The vice president says the U.S. and Iran are "very close" to a deal, but are "not there yet." Meanwhile, the U.S. struck Iran, which retaliated against a U.S. base.

item.title: Blue Origin rocket explodes on launch pad in Florida
item.description: Blue Origin, which is owned by Amazon founder Jeff Bezos, was gearing up for a June launch to put a batch of Amazon "Leo" internet satellites into orbit.

item.title: Shrey Parikh of California wins 2026 Scripps National Spelling Bee in spell-off
item.description: Shrey Parikh of Rancho Cucamonga, California, emerged victorious Thursday in the 98th annual Scripps National Spelling Bee.

item.title: Russian drone strikes Romanian apartment building, Romania says
item.description: The Russian drone struck an apartment building, wounding two people, Romanian officials said.

item.title: At least 3 killed and 5 injured in Dallas explosion, officials say
item.description: The number of victims could change as details continue to unfold and crews search through the debris.

item.title: Video shows Blue Origin rocket exploding on Florida launch pad
item.description: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket exploded on Thursday night in Cape Canaveral, Florida. "All personnel have been accounted for," Blue Origin said.

item.title: U.S. carries out new strikes against Iranian military site, official says
item.description: The U.S. military carried out another round of strikes on Iran, a U.S. official confirmed to CBS News on Wednesday, another challenge to a shaky ceasefire between the two countries.

item.title: AI-generated movie "Dreams of Violets" director on why he used AI instead of actors
item.description: "Dreams of Violets" is the first movie created entirely by artificial intelligence to debut at the Tribeca Film Festival. It's a fictional drama about five strangers who witnessed something very real, the massacre of Iranian civilians back in January. Ash Koosha, the film's director and producer, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: 5/28: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump and Iran yet to give final approval on 60-day truce, sources say; Trump administration building Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya.

item.title: Trump did not visit troops wounded in Iran war during medical center visit, sources say
item.description: When President Trump went to Walter Reed Medical Center for his six-month check-up on Tuesday, he also visited with U.S. service members. But he didn't see any of the 14 troops wounded in the war with Iran, according to a military official familiar with his visit and a family member of one of the soldiers. CBS News' Michael Kaplan reports.

item.title: What challenges await James Talarico and Ken Paxton in Texas Senate election
item.description: Texas State Rep. James Talarico and Texas Attorney General Ken Paxton will face off for a U.S. Senate seat following Paxton's primary runoff win earlier this week. CBS News political director Fin Gómez has more on the road to the midterms.

item.title: 6 of 9 bodies recovered after chemical tank implosion at paper mill
item.description: The tank ruptured Tuesday at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. facility in Longview, a city located along the southern Washington border with Oregon, killing 11 people.

item.title: Sisters of San Antonio bless the NBA's Spurs
item.description: A lot of San Antonio Spurs fans are praying the team can reach the NBA Finals this season, and some of those prayers are "professional." Tony Dokoupil has the story of four sisters from a San Antonio covenant who are heavily rooting for the Spurs.

item.title: Now that divers found 5 gold miners trapped in Laos, how will they get out?
item.description: After a grueling search, a team of elite divers found several gold miners trapped inside a mine in the Southeast Asian nation of Laos. Matt Gutman reports on the hardest part: getting them out.

item.title: Latest on Washington chemical blast
item.description: Crews continued to search Thursday for several people missing following a deadly tank incident in Longview, Washington. However, the mission has switched from rescue to recovery, and no additional survivors are expected. Carter Evans reports.

item.title: Bodycam video shows fire ripping through Oklahoma apartment complex
item.description: Police bodycam footage showed the moment as a huge fire ripped through an Oklahoma apartment complex earlier this month. Mark Strassmann has more.

item.title: FBI says it found millions in gold bars, watches, cash in former CIA official's home
item.description: In a raid earlier this month, the FBI said it found tens of millions of dollars in gold, cash and luxury goods in the home of a former CIA official. Charlie D'Agata has more on the allegations and the loot.

item.title: DOJ probing outside funding E. Jean Carroll received for Trump civil lawsuits
item.description: DOJ is probing a nonprofit run by billionaire Reid Hoffman that funded a portion of E. Jean Carroll's civil litigation against President Trump, several sources said.

item.title: Trump administration proposes NDA rule for federal employees
item.description: The Trump administration is proposing a rule for federal employees to sign non-disclosure agreements, or NDAs. CBS News legal contributor Jessica Levinson breaks it down.

item.title: 5/28: CBS Evening News
item.description: A gas explosion causes a massive fire at a Dallas apartment complex; several people are presumed dead after a Washington state chemical tank failure.

item.title: U.S. setting up Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya, officials say
item.description: The U.S. is in the process of setting up a quarantine facility in Kenya for Americans who were exposed to Ebola​ or infected with the deadly illness.

item.title: USCG can send divers to Bahamas to search for Lynette Hooker, source says
item.description: Lynette Hooker​ was reported missing by her husband in early April after the couple allegedly went for a nighttime ride aboard a dinghy.

item.title: Woman missing right hand speaks out about viral traffic stop video
item.description: Kathleen Thomas, who is missing her right hand, thought it was a misunderstanding when a deputy pulled her over for allegedly holding her phone with that hand while driving. Bodycam video shows the viral interaction.

item.title: 2 dead, 9 workers "unrecovered" after Washington chemical tank implosion
item.description: The damaged tank at Nippon Dynawave Packaging Co. held approximately 900,000 gallons of white liquor, a chemical used in paper processing, authorities said.
